So I want to show you with this ad account how this works and how we actually go about setting up and creating and launching our campaigns. So right here, there are three main tabs along the top. And then the ads are going to be the individual pieces of creative and ad copy that are getting in front of our ad set or our audiences. So ultimately, you can think of this as like, you know, different layers. We have our first layer, which is our campaign type, our campaign layer. We then have our ad set layer and then we have our ads layer. And each campaign has to have at least one ad set and then each ad set has to have at least one ad to be able to create conversions. So I'm going to go here to audiences and underneath our audiences tab, this is where we can create the different pools of individuals or people or prospects that we can actually get our ads in front of. Now, there's going to be a blue button right here, which says create audience. I want you to hit create audience and you're going to see three different options. There's a saved audience. There's a lookalike audience and there's a custom audience. So let's dive into saved audiences first. What is a saved audience? A saved audience is essentially when we are using and tapping into the data and the targeting and demographic parameters that meta allows us as advertisers to tap into and use. Now, over time, if you've ran Facebook ads in the past or meta ads in the past, you've realized that the amount of data and the amount of targeting options that we have with the saved audience has been slowly and slowly restricted, meaning that we don't have the same abilities and capabilities here to target people that we used to have. So this is really powerful in one of two ways. If we can have a custom audience of previous customers, we're giving Meta the most effective information that we possibly can. hash and upload us. We've got 5000 rows uploaded here and we're going to hit done. And now what you can see is we have this custom audience of our data that we've given Meta. Now really important here, in order for you to create a custom audience, you have to have a minimum of 100 matched contacts. So for example, let's say you've got a list of, I don't know, 100 people exactly on your email list or 100 buyers exactly. If one of those people, if the data that you upload, the email address or the phone number doesn't match a Meta account, you won't be able to create that custom audience. Best that you upload a file size of 200, 300, 500 people, the more information that you can give Meta the better because it gives you obviously a higher likelihood and probability of having a 100 people minimum match rate. profile, anyone who sent a message, anyone who saved any post or ad. I like anyone who engaged because this is essentially anyone who took action on a previous post of ours. And what we can do is we can come here and set the parameter of the number of days that has been since they've engaged with us. And you know, we can also exclude them. We can take specific actions and measures to do whatever we want with this type of data. So this is a really powerful one as well. They know literally more about us than we know about ourselves. They can predict behavior, life events, life actions better than we can even predict ourselves because they have so much information. What if there was a way that we could take our data, our custom audiences and tell Facebook, hey, go create an audience of people that most closely resemble this. Well, it's called a lookalike audience and we use them all the time. So what you do is you come here and once you create your custom audience, you go to lookalike audience and we come here and actually select the person that we're trying to target. So if I come here, SRG buyers past 30 days, this is a custom audience that I have created of all of the buyers from our site for the past 30 days. Now what we can do is come here and select the audience location so I can come here back to the United States. And then what we have right here is a percentage scale. We have 0% to 10%. Now what this is going to do is that if we toggle to 2%, you're going to see that it goes from 2.8 million people to 5.6. If we go to 3, it goes to 8.5, 4% 11.3, 5% 14.1, etc. And this amount of people is going to be relative to how big the population is in the audience location that you're targeting. But if we go all the way to 10%, you can see here, this is 28 million people that most closely resemble all of our buyers from the past 30 days. Now I would recommend that you start with a 1% lookalike. Why? Because 1% is going to be people that are most similar to your audience source. Increasing the percentage creates a bigger, broader audience. So meaning if you go 2%, it's technically more people that don't most closely resemble your buyers, etc. All the way to 10%. So best practices is to create a lookalike audience of 1% until you start to spend more money and as you scale and you get more data back into Meta, you're able to actually create larger and bigger lookalike audiences. ## [27:11 - 30:13]

You can see here that the conversion location is the website, meaning the place where we've installed the pixel. The performance goal is maximizing the number of conversions. Our pixel is sweet rolls golf and the conversion event is the purchase. So we're telling Meda exactly what we want it to get for us. We're going to leave the attribution setting as a seven day click through. We're not going to get into that for a second and a one day view through. We're going to keep everything here the same impression and standard. And then what we're going to do here is we're going to set our daily budget. I would recommend that you spend a minimum of $20 a day. And since it's your first campaign, I don't think you'll be able to spend more than probably $50 a day. So I recommend that if you do want to get more data and you want to spend more and test more and that's something that's comfortable for you to do, you would maximize your ability to spend that $50 a day. You'd hit that maximum threshold that you could spend. Now you're going to have to spend $50 a day for probably four to six weeks before Meda is going to allow you to spend more money on their platform because they want to make sure that you're compliant, you're a legitimate business, you're not spamming their community. They don't want people, random people to just go out with spamming offers and spamming millions of people spending tens and tens of millions of dollars. It can be problematic for their platform. So they have kind of these safety measures in place. But you could come here and select, I want to spend $50 a day. Now it's really important that you understand whatever you select as your daily budget is going to get spent 100%. They are never going to not spend your budget. So if you say, hey, I'm not willing to spend $50 a day, but you're putting $50 in with the hopes that they're not going to spend that, will never happen. So whatever you are willing to spend, put that daily budget here. Now I wouldn't set an end date. I would leave it just a start date and I'd come here and select the start date time. Typically we would do midnight the next day of whenever we're launching. The reason why I wouldn't set an end date is because let's say this campaign starts to perform really, really well. What happens is you're going to be in something called the learning phase for the first 50 conversion events. It takes 50 conversion events for you to actually get out of the learning phase. And if you spend money and you're optimizing and Meta's getting the results and learning off of your data and it's getting you the results that you're looking for and your campaign is a success, and then ultimately you set an end date and it stops. Sometimes it's problematic because if you want to go in and actually relaunch that campaign, you have to relaunch, set it up from scratch and reenter the learning phase. So I never set an end date because if it continues to work, I want to run this campaign for as long as I possibly can. And guess what? If it's not working, I can go back at any given point in time and turn the campaign off simply by toggling this button over to the left right here. So now we've set our daily budget.
